title: Air Fryer Prawn & Sesame Toast
summary: We're obsessed with this simple air fryer prawn toast recipe. An excellent fakeaway recipe.

method.description: Put the prawns into a bowl, then mash them with a fork. Alternatively, mash the prawns in a food processor (using a fork can be a little awkward). Grate the garlic and ginger.

Add the sesame oil, garlic and ginger to the mashed prawns. Mix well. Divide the mixture equally between the bread slices, then spread it perfectly flat with a knife. Make sure the whole slice is covered.
Cut each piece of bread into four triangles, so that you have 16 triangles in total.

Beat the egg in a small bowl. Carefully dip each triangle into the beaten egg, plain-side down. You’re only coating the bread side, not the prawn side. Transfer the dipped triangles to a plate, egg-side up and scatter with sesame seeds.
Spray each triangle, egg-side up, with oil spray and stick in the air fryer for 8 mins at 200°C.
Serve with the sweet chilli sauce on the side, for dipping. Enjoy every triangle… so so good.

chefs.name: Nathan Anthony
chefs.summary: Meet Nathan Anthony, the Northern Irish home cook behind Bored of Lunch. During lockdown, he started his food blog to others to cook and eat healthier giving everyone something positive to focus on. Specialising in easy, budget-friendly recipes, Nathan’s platform boasts over 4 million online followers and subscribers. With four bestselling cookbooks already under his belt, Nathan’s journey with Bored of Lunch has been nothing short of remarkable. Join the BOL family and explore his delicious recipes across the website, cookbooks, and social media channels.
